title: Vector-1 Flight Simulator
desc: Fly a simulated aircraft using a dual-joystick Arduino flight deck!
fullDesc: Build a custom dual-joystick flight deck controller using an Arduino Uno and analog joysticks to fly the VECTOR-1 flight simulator! This web-based 3D flight simulator uses Three.js for rendering and the Web Serial API to communicate directly with your physical controller, providing real-time cockpit displays like an artificial horizon, altitude and speed tapes, vertical speed, and G-force readouts.
components: Web Browser with Web Serial API support (Chrome/Edge)
author: Ved

codeFiles.name: flightsim_arduino.ino
codeFiles.desc: Arduino Uno sketch to read joystick values and send them via Serial.

guide: Wire Joystick 1 (Pitch/Roll) to pins A0 and A1
Wire Joystick 2 (Throttle/Yaw) to pins A2 and A3
Flash the flightsim_arduino.ino sketch onto your board
Open the VECTOR-1 simulator and click Connect Arduino
Fly the simulator using physical joystick movements!
